claim: For all integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is at most n^2.
predicate: n < 1 or sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n*n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by n.
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % n == 0

claim: For all odd integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is congruent to 1 mod 4.
predicate: n % 2 == 0 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % 4 == 1

claim: For all integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) / n is an integer less than or equal to n.
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % n == 0 and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n <= n

claim: For all prime n >= 2, sigma(n) * phi(n) equals n^2 - 1.
predicate: not is_prime(n) or sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - 1

claim: For all integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is less than n^2 unless n is 1.
predicate: n == 1 or sigma(n) * phi(n) < n*n

claim: For all integers n >= 2, sigma(n) * phi(n) / n is strictly less than n.
predicate: n < 2 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n < n

claim: For all composite n >= 4, sigma(n) * phi(n) is strictly less than n^2 - n.
predicate: n < 4 or not (n > 1 and not is_prime(n)) or sigma(n) * phi(n) < n*n - n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by tau(n).
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % tau(n) == 0

claim: For all n >= 2, sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - 2 if and only if n is not a prime power.
predicate: n < 2 or (not (n > 1 and all(p == list(factorint(n).keys())[0] for p in factorint(n).keys())) and sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n*n - 2) or (n > 1 and all(p == list(factorint(n).keys())[0] for p in factorint(n).keys()) and sigma(n) * phi(n) > n*n - 2)

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) / tau(n) is an integer.
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % tau(n) == 0

claim: For all odd composite n >= 9, sigma(n) * phi(n) is congruent to 1 mod 8.
predicate: n % 2 == 0 or n < 9 or is_prime(n) or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % 8 == 1

claim: For all n >= 1, n divides sigma(n) * phi(n) and tau(n) divides sigma(n) * phi(n).
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % n == 0 and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % tau(n) == 0

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by lcm(n, tau(n)).
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % lcm(n, tau(n)) == 0

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 if and only if n is 1 or a prime.
predicate: n < 1 or (n == 1 or is_prime(n)) == (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n)

claim: For all n >= 2, if sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2, then n is prime.
predicate: n < 2 or sigma(n) * phi(n) != n*n or is_prime(n)

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 - n if and only if n is the product of two distinct primes.
predicate: n < 1 or (n > 1 and len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values())) == (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n)

claim: For all n >= 6, if n is squarefree with exactly two prime factors, then sigma(n) * phi(n) / n = n - 1.
predicate: n < 6 or not (n > 1 and len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values())) or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n == n - 1

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is either n^2, n^2 - n, or strictly less than n^2 - n.
predicate: n < 1 or sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n or sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n or sigma(n) * phi(n) < n*n - n

claim: For all n >= 3, if n is not a prime power and not a product of two distinct primes, then sigma(n) * phi(n) < n^2 - 2n.
predicate: n < 3 or (n > 1 and not all(p == list(factorint(n).keys())[0] for p in factorint(n).keys()) and not (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values()))) and sigma(n) * phi(n) < n*n - 2*n

claim: For all n >= 1, n divides sigma(n) * phi(n) - n^2 + n.
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n) - n*n + n) % n == 0

claim: For all odd n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is congruent to 1 mod 4 if n is prime, and to 1 or 5 mod 8 if n is composite.
predicate: n % 2 == 0 or (is_prime(n) and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % 4 == 1) or (not is_prime(n) and ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % 8 == 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % 8 == 5))

claim: For all n >= 2, if n is composite then sigma(n) * phi(n) / n < n - 1.
predicate: n < 2 or is_prime(n) or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n < n - 1

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) / n is an integer and divides sigma(n) * phi(n) / tau(n).
predicate: n < 1 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % n == 0 and ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n) % ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) // tau(n)) == 0

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 - n if and only if n is squarefree and has exactly two prime factors.
predicate: n < 1 or (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values())) == (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n)

claim: For all n >= 6 that are squarefree and have exactly two prime factors, sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 - n and is divisible by 6.
predicate: n < 6 or not (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values())) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % 6 == 0)

claim: For all n >= 6 that are products of two distinct primes, n divides sigma(n) * phi(n) - n^2 + n and the result is divisible by 6.
predicate: n < 6 or not (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values())) or ((sigma(n) * phi(n) - n*n + n) % n == 0 and (sigma(n) * phi(n) - n*n + n) % 6 == 0)

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is either n^2, n^2 - n, or less than n^2 - 2n + 1.
predicate: n < 1 or sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n or sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n or sigma(n) * phi(n) < (n - 1)**2

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 if n = 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 - n if n is a product of two distinct primes, and sigma(n) * phi(n) < (n - 1)^2 otherwise.
predicate: n < 1 or (n == 1 and sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n) or (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values()) and sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n) or (not (n == 1 or (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values()))) and sigma(n) * phi(n) < (n - 1)**2)

claim: For all n >= 2, sigma(n) * phi(n) / n is integer, and equals n if n is prime, n-1 if n is a product of two distinct primes, and less than n-1 otherwise.
predicate: n < 2 or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % n == 0) and ((is_prime(n) and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n == n) or (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values()) and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n == n - 1) or (not (is_prime(n) or (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values()))) and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) // n < n - 1))

claim: For all odd n >= 9 that are products of two distinct primes, sigma(n) * phi(n) is congruent to 1 mod 8 and equals n^2 - n.
predicate: n % 2 == 0 or n < 9 or not (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values())) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % 8 == 1)

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by lcm(n, tau(n)) and equals n^2, n^2 - n, or less than (n-1)^2.
predicate: n < 1 or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % lcm(n, tau(n)) == 0) and (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n or sigma(n) * phi(n) == n*n - n or sigma(n) * phi(n) < (n - 1)**2)

claim: For all n >= 1, the difference n^2 - sigma(n)*phi(n) is at least 0, equals 0 iff n=1, equals n iff n is a product of two distinct primes, and is greater than n otherwise.
predicate: n < 1 or (n*n - sigma(n)*phi(n) == 0 and n == 1) or (n*n - sigma(n)*phi(n) == n and len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values())) or (n*n - sigma(n)*phi(n) > n and not (n == 1 or (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values()))))

claim: For all n >= 2, n divides n^2 - sigma(n)*phi(n) only if n is a product of two distinct primes.
predicate: n < 2 or (n*n - sigma(n)*phi(n)) % n == 0 implies (len(factorint(n)) == 2 and all(v == 1 for v in factorint(n).values()))

claim: For all odd n >= 1, n^2 - sigma(n)*phi(n) is even if n is composite, and odd if n is prime.
predicate: n % 2 == 0 or (is_prime(n) and (n*n - sigma(n)*phi(n)) % 2 == 1) or (not is_prime(n) and (n*n - sigma(n)*phi(n)) % 2 == 0)

claim: For all n >= 1, if n^2 - sigma(n)*phi(n) > n, then n has at least three distinct prime factors or is divisible by p^2 for some prime p.
predicate: n < 1 or (n*n - sigma(n)*phi(n) > n) implies (len(factorint(n)) >= 3 or any(v > 1 for v in factorint(n).values()))
